results.title: Pareto multi-objective optimization of metro train energy-saving operation using improved NSGA-II algorithms - ScienceDirect
excerpts:
Section Title: Pareto multi-objective optimization of metro train energy-saving operation using improved NSGA-II...
Content:
Author links open overlay panel Zhenyu Zhang a b , Xiaoqing Cheng b , Zongyi Xing a , Xingdong Gui a

Share

https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chaos.2023.114183 [Get rights and content](https://s100.copyright.com/AppDispatchServlet?publisherName=ELS&contentID=S0960077923010858&orderBeanReset=true)
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Introduction
Content:
Non-dominated sorting genetic algorithm-II (NSGA-II) has the advantages of fast running speed, good convergence of solution set, good diversity of the population in the iterative process, and high accuracy of optimization results [16,17]. It is widely used in energy strategy optimization problems [[18], [19], [20]]. Therefore, we develop an energy-saving operation optimization method for metro trains based on an improved NSGA-II algorithm. The contributions of this paper are provided as follows. Firstly, the objective functions and constraints in the energy-saving optimization models are presented after analyzing the train operation indexes. Secondly, the improved NSGA-II algorithm with non-dominated sorting (NDS), congestion design, and elitist strategy is used to solve the energy-saving optimization models.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Conclusions
Content:
The energy-saving optimization model with a fixed travel time of train operation is developed in this paper. Firstly, a train energy-saving operation optimization model is established by combining energy consumption and punctuality. Then, the solution method based on the Pareto archive NSGA-II algorithm is developed. Finally, the developed optimization model is applied in Guangzhou Metro Line 7 to verify the performance. The results show that the energy consumption of the developed
 ...

excerpts:
Section Title: A Pareto-based genetic algorithm for online task allocation in mobile crowdsensing
Content:
Author links open overlay panel Xiangling Wu , Wenming Ma , Xiao Zhu , Shengyang Sun , Xiaoang Zhu

Share

https://doi.org/10.1016/j.comcom.2025.108269 [Get rights and content](https://s100.copyright.com/AppDispatchServlet?publisherName=ELS&contentID=S0140366425002269&orderBeanReset=true)
 ... 
Section Title: A Pareto-based genetic algorithm for online task allocation in mobile crowdsensing > Abstract
Content:
To solve this problem, we offer an enhanced approach that is based on a genetic algorithm and uses a bipartite network to account for the assignability link between participants and tasks. Additionally, we employ Pareto optimization to balance the dual objectives of minimizing reward and maximizing task quality. Results from experiments show that the suggested algorithm works better than baseline methods in many experimental configurations.
 ... 
Section Title: A Pareto-based genetic algorithm for online task allocation in mobile crowdsensing > Introduction
Content:
The extensive range of location-based tasks [11] expands the problem’s solution space significantly, while the diverse constraints imposed by tasks and participants further escalate the problem’s complexity [12]. Multi-task allocation in crowdsensing, involving a large pool of mobile users, represents a significant optimization challenge [13].
 ... 
Section Title: A Pareto-based genetic algorithm for online task allocation in mobile crowdsensing > Introduction
Content:
(2) Achieving an optimal allocation scheme is challenging when the available participants and tasks to be assigned fluctuate under various constraints. We chose a genetic algorithm and modified the corresponding crossover and mutation operations to address the real-time task allocation problem. However, designing an appropriate chromosome representation for the scenario considered in this study, as well as balancing the objectives of minimizing rewards and maximizing quality, presents a significant challenge.
The main achievements of this paper are stated below.
(1) In this paper, we address an online heterogeneous task allocation problem with time constraints on both participants and tasks, while balancing task rewards and sensing quality.
Section Title: A Pareto-based genetic algorithm for online task allocation in mobile crowdsensing > Introduction
Content:
(2) We analyze the allocability relationship between participants and tasks using a bipartite graph model and apply Pareto front optimization to balance task rewards and sensing quality. Building on this, we propose the Bipartite Pareto Genetic Algorithm (BPGA), an improved genetic algorithm tailored for online task allocation scenarios.
(3) We assess the suggested algorithms based on synthetic with several baseline algorithms. The experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our methods over baseline algorithms.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > Offline task allocation
Content:
Offline task allocation refers to the scenario where all information about tasks and participants is known in advance, allowing the system to optimize the allocation based on overall information.
Shen et al. [16] proposed a constrained multi-objective optimization model for the variable-speed multi-task assignment problem, aiming to improve both the convergence and distribution of the solution. Wang et al. [17] enhanced assignment robustness by considering stochastic disturbances and selecting

results.title: …   NSGA -II to solve a novel  multi - objective task allocation  …
Pareto Optimization in Multi-Objective Planning - Emergent Mind
excerpts:
Section Title: Pareto Optimization in Multi-Objective Planning > 1. Formal Models and Pareto Dominance in Planning
Content:
A multi-objective planning problem is typically formalized as a tuple ( X , { f 1 ​ , … , f m ​ }) , where X is the feasible plan set and each f i ​ : X → R is an objective function to be minimized (or maximized, via sign convention). The Pareto dominance relation, defined as
x ≺ y ⟺ ∀ i , f i ​ ( x ) ≤ f i ​ ( y ) ∧ ∃ j , f j ​ ( x ) < f j ​ ( y ) ,
induces the Pareto front as the set of non-dominated solutions.
Section Title: Pareto Optimization in Multi-Objective Planning > 1. Formal Models and Pareto Dominance in Planning
Content:
In advanced planning domains such as robotics with temporal tasks, the optimization may involve both trajectory-level and task-level objective vectors. For example, given a robot operating in a weighted transition system and an assignment of multiple co-safe [LTL](https://www.emergentmind.com/topics/linear-temporal-logic-ltl-formula) tasks, objectives may include the cumulative task satisfaction costs (preference cost vector) and a global resource cost, with dominance defined over plan objective tuples ( J pref ​ ( π ) , J cost ​ ( π )) ( Amorese et al., 2023 ).
In multi-agent coordination, the objective vector may include arrival times for each agent, joint task completion costs, or risk profiles, with Pareto optimality representing solutions where no agent’s metric can be improved without deteriorating another ( Zhao et al., 2018 ).
 ... 
Section Title: Pareto Optimization in Multi-Objective Planning > 3. Domains of Application and Empirical Results
Content:
**Robotic Task and Motion Planning:** Pareto methods are used to synthesize resource- and preference-optimal plans for robots completing sets of temporal tasks, balancing speed or energy with variable user-defined completion preferences ( Amorese et al., 2023 ). In multi-robot systems, Pareto fronts represent the trade-off surface of arrival times or resource usages across agents ( Zhao et al., 2018 ). **AI Planning Benchmarks:** Large-scale AI planning domains are addressed by evolutionary multi-objective algorithms (e.g., Pareto-based Divide-and-Evolve) that outperform aggregation-based planners in coverage and uniformity of solution trade-offs ( Khouadjia et al., 2013 ). New benchmarks such as MultiZenoTravel provide constructive ground-truth Pareto fronts for rigorous algorithm validation ( Quemy et al., 2023 ).
 ... 
Section Title: Pareto Optimization in Multi-Objective Planning > 3. Domains of Application and Empirical Results
Content:
**Mixed-Traffic and Behavioral Planning:** Application to lane-change optimization with comfort, safety, and efficiency objectives for ego and surrounding vehicles demonstrates the impact of Pareto-NSGA-II-based planners in real and simulated traffic, yielding significant total cost reductions ( Li et al., 2021 ).
